steps.title: Find the key contrast signal
steps.content: The word "but" signals that the new archaeological evidence does something that opposes the scholars' long-held belief. The belief: silk spread beyond China in the second century CE. The evidence: people in the Indus Civilization used silk at least 1,000 years earlier. The blank must describe evidence that directly overturns an established view.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — investigated: Evidence can't investigate a belief; only researchers investigate. ✗
  • Choice B — misinterpreted: Evidence can't misinterpret a belief; only people can misinterpret. ✗
  • Choice C — anticipated: New evidence can't predict a belief that already existed; this reverses the timeline. ✗
  • Choice D — contradicted: Contradicted means directly opposed or disproved. New evidence showing silk use 1,000 years before the previously accepted date directly disproves the scholars' timeline. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
